title: Too long RUN instruction should be split into multiple lines
description: Too long RUN instruction should be split into multiple linesAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellHaving longRUNinstruction on a single line reduce the visibility and make it harder to read and understand.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIn Dockerfiles,RUNinstruction tend to be extensive and can rapidly become quite long, containing a lot of instruction with parameters and flags.In order to keep a good readability and help for future maintenance, it is important to split them into multiple lines.It is usually a good idea to group elements that belong together on the same lines.The usage of the operator&&allows to chain multiple operators. Also, the default operator\allows to continue theRUNinstruction on the next line.Another way is to use the here
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Too long RUN instruction should be split into multiple lines. Long RUN instructions on a single line reduce readability and make maintenance harder. Split the instruction across multiple lines using backslash (\) continuation or && operators to improve readability. Consider keeping each logical command on its own line.
